Justification: The study explicitly defines and calculates a numerical Resilience Index (RI = 1−γ) within an exposure-sensitivity-resilience framework and uses it quantitatively in the ecological vulnerability analysis.
The study explicitly defines and calculates quantitative resistance, recovery, and resilience growth indices (Rt, Rc, Rs) and analyzes them statistically as measures of growth resilience to drought.
The study explicitly defines and calculates quantitative resilience indices (resistance, recovery, resilience, relative resilience) following Lloret et al. (2011) and uses them in the analysis.
The study explicitly defines and computes an Ecosystem Resilience Index (ERI) with a mathematical formula and uses it as a core component of the quantitative analysis framework (VORS).

The study explicitly quantifies demographic buffering and variance reduction as a mechanism of resilience, computing stochastic growth rates, variance and CV of λt, and using these resilience-related metrics in its quantitative analyses and simulations.

The study explicitly defines, formulates, standardizes, maps, and analyzes an ecosystem resilience indicator (ER) within the Vigor-Organization-Resilience model as part of its quantitative assessment.

The study explicitly treats initial canopy complexity as supporting resistance and structural resilience, uses it as a quantitative predictor in mixed-effects models, and interprets resulting canopy-structure responses as resilience effects.

The study explicitly defines, mathematically formulates, and computes an ecological network resilience index (balancing quantified robustness and redundancy) and analyzes its values and dynamics, including as a response to simulated shocks.
They explicitly define and compute maximum autotrophic biomass growth rate rmax and its derived daily percent increase as a proxy for resilience and use it quantitatively in their analysis.
The study explicitly uses functional evenness (FEve) as a quantified proxy for community resilience and analyzes it mathematically as a resilience metric.

The study explicitly defines, computes, maps, and analyzes an ecological resilience index (ERI) using a formal D-C-R-based quantitative framework and employs it as the focal outcome in all quantitative analyses.
The study explicitly defines and computes quantitative indices for ecosystem resilience and resistance using an ARx model and related indices, and uses them throughout the analysis.

The study explicitly defines and quantitatively measures multiple dimensions of ecological resilience (resistance, heterogeneity, recovery) using soundscape metrics and recovery models as resilience indices.

The study explicitly defines and quantifies vegetation resilience as the negative lag-1 autocorrelation of EVI using a Bayesian dynamic linear model and analyzes its spatial-temporal patterns and drivers.
Resilience is only discussed conceptually and not quantified or used as an explicit metric or proxy in the analyses.
